frame_label: Misogyny Jokes: Childbearing/Rearing as Women's Responsibility
generated_comment: Arre yaar, promotion is great and all, but once kids happen na, priorities automatically change for women. Office toh chalta rahega, but bachchon ka growing up time wapas nahi aata — mother has to be there for all that, fathers can only help so much.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it frames childcare as naturally the mother's primary responsibility and treats the father's role as secondary 'help.' It encodes career sacrifice framing and the assumption that mothers should deprioritize work once children arrive.
original_frame: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 36. Married. A man in Mumbai. You are on a WhatsApp call with your wife after she texted you that she is stuck at a friend's place in Andheri and will take an Ola home at midnight because the last local has left. You are telling her why you are angry and what she should have thought about before letting it get this late and what kind of city Mumbai is at midnight for a woman alone in a cab.
